LanguageData.ConfirmGravEngineLaunch:
Are you sure you want to launch?
Your current map, as well as anything not on Hellion deck plating, will be permanently lost. The launch process is destructive, meaning you will not be able to return.
LanguageData.PawnsKidnappedOnGravship: The following people and animals from another faction will be taken with you. This will upset their faction, potentially turning them hostile.
LanguageData.BuildingsWillBeLeftBehind: The following buildings are not fully on the Hellion and will be left behind
LanguageData.SubstructureWillBeLeftBehind: Some deck plating has invalid placement and will be left behind
LanguageData.NamePlayerGravshipMessage: {0} has high hopes for your rebuilt Hellion and wants to christen it with a proper name. What should the aircraft be called?
LanguageData.PlayerGravshipGainsName: The Hellion is now known as {0}.
LanguageData.OrbitalWarning_Gravship: Prepare the Hellion for orbit: - Use airtight walls and doors. - Ensure rooms are well heated and fully roofed. - Construct oxygen pumps.
LanguageData.CommandShowSubstructureOverlayDesc: Display valid Hellion deck plating in blue and invalid plating in red.
LanguageData.CommandInspectGravEngineDesc: Choose a colonist to inspect the mysterious Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.CommandLaunchGravshipDesc: Choose a colonist to pilot the Hellion. Anything on Hellion deck plating will be transported to a new map. Your current map will be abandoned if you do not have a mooring mast.
LanguageData.ConfirmLandGravshipDesc: Land the Hellion in the landing zone.
LanguageData.AbortLandGravshipDesc:
Cancel landing on this map, and force the Hellion to land nearby on the world map. Useful if you cannot find a landing spot.
Warning: This increases the chance of a mishap when landing.
LanguageData.AbortLandConfirmation:
Aborting the landing will force your Hellion to land in a random nearby location on the world map. This increases the chance of a mishap when landing.
Are you sure you want to continue?
LanguageData.GravshipLandingMarkerNotPlaced: You must designate a landing area for the Hellion.
LanguageData.GravshipShieldOrderActivationDesc: Order a pawn to activate the Hellion shield projector.
LanguageData.GravshipShieldOrderDeactivationDesc: Order a pawn to deactivate the Hellion shield projector.
LanguageData.DesignatorRemoveFoundationDesc: Remove bridges or Hellion deck plating to reveal the terrain underneath.
LanguageData.RequiresSignalJammer: Heavy weapons systems: Requires ECM suite to approach
LanguageData.GravshipOutcomeChanceLetterExtra: Based on the Hellion's launch, there was a {0_percentage} chance of a negative outcome.
LanguageData.PilotConsoleInaccessible: Cockpit console is not accessible from the Hellion
LanguageData.CaveEntranceLetterText:
The reactor-core signal is coming from a nearby cave network. The entrance is guarded by insect hives.
Explore the cave network to find the reactor core.
LanguageData.LetterAncientGravEngineDiscovered: You have discovered an ancient Hellion reactor. Destroy it to obtain a reactor core.
LanguageData.GravshipShieldTip: A short-lived repulsor screen that blocks projectiles. It works against normal shots and mortars. It shuts down instantly if hit by an EMP.
LanguageData.MessageMustBePlacedOnSubstructure: Must be placed on Hellion deck plating.
LanguageData.MessageMustBePlacedInRangeOfGravEngineOr: Must be placed within the radius of a Hellion reactor or airframe extender.
LanguageData.MessageCannotPlaceOverSubstructure: Cannot be placed on Hellion deck plating.
LanguageData.MessageMustBePlacedInRangeOfGravEngine: Must be placed within range of a Hellion reactor.
LanguageData.MessageConfirmLandingAreaFirst: Confirm Hellion landing zone to continue.
LanguageData.GravcoreAbandonedSettlementRaid: {FACTION_pawnsPlural} from {FACTION_name} are attacking. They used the reactor core to lure you into an ambush.
LanguageData.GravEngineInspectedLetterContents:
Among the remnants of the crashed robot platform, a still-intact Hellion reactor hums with energy.
You can build Hellion deck plating around the reactor to create a new aircraft. The assembly sustains the Hellion's lift envelope and allows long-distance travel.
Launch the Hellion by researching Hellion airframe, then building fuel tanks, VTOL thrusters, and a cockpit console.
Launching the Hellion will abandon your current map unless you build a mooring mast first.
- Research unlocked: Hellion airframe - Flooring unlocked: Hellion deck plating
LanguageData.AsteroidLetterText:
You've discovered the location of an asteroid orbiting the planet. It contains significant amounts of {RESOURCE}, and potentially other resources.
Orbital miners steer valuable asteroids into planetary orbit so that they can be easily mined and sold to orbital traders. You do not detect any signs of life from this asteroid.
You can reach the asteroid with cargo bots, vertibirds, or the Hellion.
LanguageData.AncientReactorArrivedLetterText: An ancient reactor is hidden within nearby ruins. The reactor is damaged and leaking, covering the region in toxic fallout. Locate and disable the reactor to obtain a reactor core and stop the fallout.
LanguageData.FrozenTerraformerArrivedLetterText: The malfunctioning terraformer has encased itself in solid ice. The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within. Find and destroy the terraformer to obtain the reactor core.
LanguageData.AncientStockpileArrivedLetterText:
The reactor-core signal is coming from one of the nearby underground stockpiles. Hack the entrances and search the stockpiles to find the reactor core.
Be careful, some of the defense systems may still be active.
LanguageData.OrbitalMechanoidPlatformLetterArrivedTex:
This orbital platform is built to service and deploy combat robots, likely as part of some ancient planet-spanning war machine.
The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within.
LanguageData.OrbitalAncientPlatformLetterArrivedText:
The people who once inhabited this orbital platform are long gone and its original purpose has long been forgotten.
The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within.
LanguageData.CrashedPlatformArrivedLetterText:
The remains of an orbital platform are strewn nearby. Be careful, the wreckage contains hostile robots.
The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere within the crash site.
LanguageData.LetterTextMechanoidThreat:
The robot command hub's scanning process is close to determining your location. When it locates the Hellion, it will send a massive robot force to kill you.
Gather your supplies, then flee the map by selecting the cockpit console and selecting launch.
LanguageData.LetterTextMechhiveWarning:
You have detected a massive Hellion-systems energy reading indicating that the robot command hub is hidden somewhere in orbit. If you could find it, you could shut it down - or make use of its power.
Robot command hubs are production-and-control platforms that manufacture robots and coordinate their actions around the planet. They are powered by a persona core.
Collect more Hellion components to track down the robot command hub's location.
LanguageData.LetterGravshipEnteredEnemyBase: Your Hellion has landed at {0} and is ready to begin the attack.
LanguageData.EnteredMegahiveGravcoreExtra: The reactor-core signal is coming from somewhere behind the burrow walls. Attack the burrow walls to explore further.
LanguageData.StatsReport_GravshipRangeOffset_Desc: How much further this building lets the Hellion travel on the world map.
LanguageData.StatsReport_GravshipFuelSavings_Desc: How much this building reduces fuel needed for Hellion travel.
LanguageData.StatsReport_GravEngineFuelConsumption_De: A multiplier on how much fuel is needed during Hellion travel.
